Science may not have the plates. But science cannot produce eleven independent witnesses who handled an object and died testifying to its reality.

missionary.simple: Joseph returned the plates to the angel Moroni when the translation was complete. But eleven men physically handled them and testified under their own names. Not one of them ever denied it.
missionary.explain: 'Three witnesses heard the voice of God declare the translation true. Eight more handled the plates with their hands. Many left the Church under bitter circumstances — and still none of them denied seeing the plates.'
missionary.why: In any court of law, eleven independent witnesses who maintained their testimony under pressure would establish a fact beyond reasonable doubt.
missionary.invite: Would you read the testimonies of the witnesses at the front of the Book of Mormon and ask God whether their testimony is true?

questions.question: 165. Why does the Book of Mormon contain DNA and archaeological inconsistencies regarding Native American origins?
questions.answer:
The Book of Mormon does not claim that Lehi's family were the sole ancestors of all Native Americans. It records the history of a small family who crossed the ocean and settled in the Americas around 600 BC. They arrived on a continent that already had inhabitants. Their descendants mixed with existing peoples over the following centuries.

The genetic signal of one small family from the Near East, introduced into a population of millions over thousands of years, would be virtually undetectable in modern DNA studies — especially with the limited tools currently available for ancient population genetics. Finding a Semitic genetic signature in a broad population sample is not a reasonable expectation. Scientists who have studied ancient Near Eastern DNA acknowledge that the field is still in its early stages.

Archaeological evidence is more layered than a surface reading suggests. The Book of Mormon describes civilisations in the Americas — roads, cities, metalworking, complex social structures — and archaeologists have confirmed all of these in pre-Columbian American cultures. The cities described in the Book of Mormon existed. The time periods match. The cultural elements align.

The specific items often raised — horses, steel, and silk — have been addressed. Archaeological evidence for pre-Columbian horses has been found in America. Steel refers to a worked metal that ancient Americans produced. Silk refers to a fine fabric produced from plants or insects — not exclusively the Asian silkworm product. The translation reflects approximations from an ancient language into 19th-century English.

No archaeologist or geneticist has disproved the Book of Mormon. What some have said is that certain things have not yet been confirmed. That is a very different claim, and it is one that honest inquiry should keep in mind.

missionary.simple: The Book of Mormon does not claim Lehi's family were the only people in the Americas. They arrived on a continent already inhabited. Their genetic signal in a large mixed population over millennia would be extremely small.
missionary.explain: 'No scientist has disproved the Book of Mormon. Some have said certain things have not yet been confirmed. That is very different from proof it is false. : the evidence is still developing.'
missionary.why: The Book of Mormon invites you to test it spiritually — not just archaeologically. God promised to answer a sincere prayer about it.
missionary.invite: Will you apply Moroni's test — read it sincerely and ask God if it is true — rather than waiting for scientists to decide for you?

questions.question: 166. What were the Kinderhook Plates, and why did Joseph Smith attempt to translate them if they were a hoax?
questions.answer:
The Kinderhook Plates were six brass plates dug up in Illinois in 1843. Joseph Smith examined them and reportedly said that the plates described a descendant of Ham through the loins of Pharaoh. In 1980, metallurgical analysis confirmed that the plates were 19th-century forgeries — a fact we acknowledge.

What is often overlooked is that Joseph Smith never claimed to formally translate the Kinderhook Plates. He made a brief comment — possibly an impression, possibly based on a partial examination. He did not produce a translation. He did not publish a revelation about them. The brief journal entry attributed to him is not in his handwriting and may have been recorded by a scribe based on a casual remark.

Compare this to the Book of Mormon — over 500 pages translated in approximately 80 days, dictated without notes or reference materials, in the presence of witnesses, covering more than 2,000 years of history. That is a very different claim than a passing comment about unverified plates.

The Kinderhook Plates did not produce scripture. They did not produce doctrine. They produced one comment in a journal. If Joseph Smith had claimed to formally translate them and published the results, the argument would have more force. He did not. A prophet can examine an artefact and make an incorrect impression about it without that undermining the validity of his prophetic calling. Moses struck the rock twice when commanded once. David numbered Israel against God's will. Prophets are not infallible in every act. The test is their fruits and their confirmed prophecies — and on that test Joseph Smith stands.

questions.question: 167. If polygamy was a commandment, why does the Book of Mormon explicitly condemn it?
questions.answer:
This question has a clear answer in the very passage some readers cite. Jacob 2:27–30 condemns polygamy under specific conditions and permits it under another. "For there shall not any man among you have save it be one wife; and concubines he shall have none... For if I will raise up seed unto me, I will command my people; otherwise they shall hearken unto these things."

The Lord gave a conditional statement. When He does not command it — the default is one wife. When He commands it to raise up a righteous seed — it is sanctioned. Both statements are in the same passage. Reading only verses 27 and 28 without verse 30 misses the full picture.

The polygamy condemned in Jacob 2 was the unauthorized, lust-driven polygamy of men who were using David and Solomon as an excuse to satisfy carnal desires — before any divine command had been given. The Lord condemned that. When He later gave the command to Joseph Smith, the situation was different — it was a divine command, given with conditions, for a specific purpose, with divine sanction.

This is the same God who gave David his wives through Nathan the prophet. 2 Samuel 12:8 The same God who permitted Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob to take plural wives. The same God who said He does not change. When He commands, it is righteous. When men do it without a command, it is condemned. The Book of Mormon condemns the latter. The Doctrine and Covenants commands the former. There is no contradiction.

missionary.simple: Jacob 2 condemns unauthorized polygamy driven by lust, and permits it when God commands it to raise up a righteous people. Both statements are in the same passage. You must read the whole thing.
missionary.explain: 'The same God who gave David his wives through Nathan the prophet also condemned David when he took Uriah's wife without divine authorization. The difference is not the practice — it is whether God commanded it.'
missionary.why: God's commandments are not arbitrary. When He gives a specific command for a specific purpose, it is right. When men act without His command, it is condemned.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to read Jacob 2 in full — all of it including verse 30 — and ask God what it is actually teaching?

questions.question: 190. How do you explain the lack of archaeological evidence for the great battles in the Book of Mormon?
questions.answer:
The absence of confirmed evidence is not the same as disproof. The civilisations described in the Book of Mormon are consistent with what archaeology has uncovered in Mesoamerica. The question is whether the specific battle sites have been identified.

Consider: the Battle of Thermopylae was one of the most famous battles in history — 300 Spartans against thousands of Persians. For centuries, only the written record confirmed it. Archaeological confirmation came slowly and incompletely. Equally, the great battles of ancient Mesoamerica — some of the most destructive in pre-Columbian history — have left remarkably little identifiable battlefield evidence despite extensive archaeological study.

Organic weapons — wooden spears, obsidian blades, leather armour — decompose. They leave no trace after millennia in tropical soil. Without the massive stone fortifications of European medieval battle sites, ancient American battlefields are virtually indistinguishable from ordinary occupation sites.

The absence of a confirmed battlefield does not mean the battle did not happen. It means we have not found it — or that the evidence has decomposed. The Book of Mormon describes a civilisation whose cities, roads, temples, and cultural patterns align with what archaeology has uncovered in Mesoamerica. The framework is confirmed. The specific battlefield locations remain to be found.

questions.question: 191. Why does the Book of Mormon mention horses, steel, and silk if they didn't exist in ancient America?
questions.answer:
These are fair questions, and each deserves a careful answer. The evidence is more nuanced than it may first appear.

Horses. Pre-Columbian horse fossils have been found in North America. The horse became extinct in the Americas around 10,000 BC — but whether it survived in isolated populations into the time period covered by the Book of Mormon is actively debated. Recent finds in Yucatan have pushed the confirmed extinction date significantly later than previously believed. The issue is not settled.

Steel. The Book of Mormon uses the word to describe worked, hardened metal. Pre-Columbian Mesoamerican cultures worked various metals including meteoric iron and copper alloys hardened by specific processes. Whether the translation word "steel" perfectly represents the ancient material is a translation question, not an archaeological one. Joseph Smith translated the concept as best he could into 19th-century English.

Silk. Multiple pre-Columbian cultures produced fine, silk-like fabrics from plants — including wild silk moths native to the Americas — and from fibres like the yucca plant. Calling these fabrics "silk" in translation is consistent with the translator's task of conveying meaning across language and time.

It is worth remembering that the English words in the Book of Mormon do not have to correspond exactly to their 21st-century scientific definitions. Translation does not work that way. The Book of Mormon is a translation of an ancient record into 19th-century English. The translator used the closest available equivalents for what he saw.

missionary.simple: Pre-Columbian America had horses, worked metals, and fine fabrics — some confirmed by archaeology, some still debated. Translation also involves approximating ancient concepts into modern language.
missionary.explain: 'Pre-Columbian horse fossils have been found in North America with dates later than previously assumed. Translation means finding the closest available word — not always the perfect scientific equivalent.'
missionary.why: No archaeologist has disproved the Book of Mormon. Honest inquiry finds that the evidence is still developing, not settled against it.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to approach the Book of Mormon as God's word first — asking Him directly — rather than waiting for every archaeological question to be resolved?

questions.question: 192. What is the Joseph Smith Translation of the Bible, and why don't you use it exclusively?
questions.answer:
Joseph Smith was commanded by the Lord to work through the Bible and make corrections and additions as directed by the Holy Ghost. He completed much of this work between 1830 and 1833. What resulted is called the Joseph Smith Translation — also known as the Inspired Version.

We use it. The standard edition of our King James Bible includes thousands of footnotes referencing the Joseph Smith Translation. Selections from it are included in our scriptures — including significant additions to Genesis now found in the Pearl of Great Price and additions to Matthew's account of Christ's teachings.

We do not use it exclusively because it is not a finished work. Joseph Smith indicated he wanted to continue revising it. His death in 1844 left it incomplete. The RLDS Church — now the Community of Christ — published the full manuscript as their canonical Bible. We have accepted the portions Joseph Smith included in our canon and use the rest as a valuable inspired resource.

Beyond the completion question — the King James Bible remains the translation most familiar to the English-speaking Christian world and most directly connected to the Greek and Hebrew manuscripts it was drawn from. The Joseph Smith Translation corrects important errors and restores important truths. But using it alongside the King James Bible, rather than replacing it, preserves the connection to the original manuscript tradition while accessing the inspired corrections.

missionary.simple: The Joseph Smith Translation is an inspired revision of the Bible that Joseph Smith completed under divine direction — restoring many plain and precious truths that were lost or obscured.
missionary.explain: 'We use it. Thousands of footnotes in our scriptures reference it. It is not finished — Joseph died before completing it — but what exists restores important truths that the Bible itself shows were once present.'
missionary.why: God did not leave His children with a corrupted or incomplete record without providing something to restore what was lost.
missionary.invite: Would you like to see a specific example of what the Joseph Smith Translation restores and ask God whether it reveals something important?

questions.question: 193. How do you view other lost scriptures or the Apocrypha?
questions.answer:
Joseph Smith received a revelation specifically about the Apocrypha. The Lord told him, "Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you concerning the Apocrypha — There are many things contained therein that are true, and it is mostly translated correctly; there are many things contained therein that are not true, which are interpolations by the hands of men. Therefore, whoso readeth it, let him understand, for the Spirit manifesteth truth." (Doctrine and Covenants 91:1–4.)

That is our answer in the Lord's own words. The Apocrypha contains truth. It contains additions by men. The Holy Ghost can help the reader distinguish between them. We do not dismiss it. We treat it with discernment.

As for other lost books — the Book of Jasher, the Book of the Wars of the Lord, Paul's letter to the Laodiceans — we believe they existed, we believe they contained truth, and we accept that the restoration of all things means that all truth will eventually be restored. Some of what was lost has been restored through the Book of Mormon and the Doctrine and Covenants. More will come as the Lord sees fit.

The Bible itself mentions books we do not have. That fact — preserved within scripture itself — is one of the strongest arguments for additional scripture that exists. The Lord did not intend us to have only what was arbitrarily preserved in one collection. He intends us to have every word that proceeds from His mouth.

questions.question: 3. Why do Latter-day Saints believe Joseph Smith was a prophet when many people rejected him in his time?
questions.answer:
Rejection alone has never been the measure of a false prophet. Christ himself was rejected. So was Moses. So was every prophet God sent. In Matthew 23:37 Christ said, "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets and stonest them which are sent unto thee." The pattern of rejection is consistent throughout scripture — and it tells us nothing about the truth of the message.

Moses gave us the divine test. If a man speaks in the name of the Lord and the thing follows, he is a prophet. Deuteronomy 18:21–22 On December 25, 1832, Joseph Smith prophesied that a great war would begin in South Carolina. Twenty-nine years before the first shot was fired at Fort Sumter, he named the very state. That was the Civil War — the most destructive war this nation had ever seen. The prophecy came to pass exactly as spoken.

In 1842, with his people at their height of prosperity in Nauvoo, Illinois, he prophesied they would be driven to the Rocky Mountains — a territory that a sitting US Senator had called worthless. He said some of them would live to help build a mighty people there. Every part of that prophecy was fulfilled.

Christ said you will know them by their fruits. The fruits of this work are before the world. Millions of lives changed, families sealed, scripture restored, and truth declared. A corrupt tree cannot bring forth good fruit. Matthew 7:16–20

Joseph Smith himself recorded the divine promise given him from the beginning: "Your name shall be had for good and evil among all nations."Joseph Smith—History 1:33 That prophecy has been fulfilled. The Book of Mormon he brought forth, the Church he organised, and the doctrine he restored stand as the fruits by which he is judged.

missionary.simple: Every prophet in history was rejected in his time — including Moses, Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Christ Himself. Rejection is not evidence of falsehood. Fulfilled prophecy is.
missionary.explain: Joseph prophesied the Civil War by name — South Carolina — 29 years before it began. He prophesied the gathering to the Rocky Mountains when a US Senator called that land worthless. Both came to pass exactly.
missionary.why: The test Moses gave us is simple: if a prophet speaks and it comes to pass, he is of God. Apply that test to Joseph Smith.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to learn about two or three of Joseph Smith's fulfilled prophecies and ask God what that means?

questions.question: 39. Why do you believe Joseph Smith saw God the Father and Jesus Christ? Isn't that impossible?
questions.answer:
Moses saw God. Exodus 33:11 Isaiah saw the Lord sitting on His throne. Isaiah 6:1 Ezekiel saw visions of God. Ezekiel 1:1 Stephen, full of the Holy Ghost, “looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God.”Acts 7:55 If God showed himself to these men, why is it impossible that He showed Himself — God the Father and His Son Jesus Christ — to Joseph Smith?

The God of the Bible appears to His servants. That is a consistent pattern from Genesis to Revelation. The claim that He stopped appearing after the first century is not found anywhere in scripture. It is a tradition of men — not the word of God.

Moses, while giving Israel the divine test for a prophet, said: “If there be a prophet among you, I the Lord will make myself known unto him in a vision, and will speak unto him in a dream.”Numbers 12:6 A vision of God is not a disqualification for a prophet. It is one of the very qualifications Moses listed.

Joseph Smith said he saw a pillar of light above the brightness of the sun, and within it two personages — one pointing to the other and saying, "This is my beloved Son. Hear Him." That testimony fulfills every qualification Moses laid out. And the fruits of his ministry — millions of lives, restored scripture, fulfilled prophecy — are exactly what Christ said would identify a true prophet.

Joseph recorded what he heard. The Father pointed to the Son and said: "This is My Beloved Son. Hear Him!"Joseph Smith—History 1:17 Two separate personages. One pointing to the other. That single encounter answered the deepest question of theology. And the Book of Mormon confirms the same embodied God: "He dwelleth in flesh."Mosiah 15:2

🕮 Scripture Evidence: God Has Been Seen

  • Moses — Exodus 33:11: The LORD spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend.
  • Moses — Exodus 33:18-23: Moses is permitted to see God's back, confirming a directional, tangible presence.
  • Moses & the elders — Exodus 24:9-11: Moses, Aaron, and seventy elders saw the God of Israel and ate and drank in His presence.
  • Jacob — Genesis 32:30: “I have seen God face to face, and yet my life is preserved.”
  • Isaiah — Isaiah 6:1-5: Isaiah beheld the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up.
  • Ezekiel — Ezekiel 1:26-28: Ezekiel describes the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD as a human form.
  • Abraham — Genesis 18:1-2: The LORD appeared to Abraham in visible, tangible form at the oaks of Mamre.
  • Job — Job 42:5: “My eyes have seen you.”

missionary.simple: Because the God of the Bible appears to His servants. Moses saw God. Stephen saw God. Paul saw Christ. The claim that God stopped appearing after the first century is a tradition of men — not scripture.
missionary.explain: Moses said: if there is a prophet, God will make Himself known in a vision. Joseph Smith's vision meets every qualification Moses listed. The question is not whether God can appear — He said He would.
missionary.why: A God who appeared to prophets for thousands of years and then suddenly stopped is not the same God. The restored gospel brings back the God of the Bible.
missionary.invite: Would you read Joseph Smith's account of the First Vision and ask God if it is true?

questions.question: 103. Why do you say the Church was "restored" and not "reformed"? Isn't that just a semantic difference?
questions.answer:
It is not just a matter of wording. The distinction is significant and worth understanding clearly.

A reform takes something that still exists and corrects its errors. Luther reformed — he took the existing Catholic Church, rejected some of its practices, and built a new version of it. Calvin reformed. Every Protestant denomination is a reform of something that came before it. But you cannot reform what no longer exists. You cannot restore authority that was not lost.

If the authority was never lost, then every church here today has a valid claim to it through some line of succession — and we are all equally legitimate. But if the authority was lost — as the scriptures Paul, Peter, and John clearly prophesied it would be — then reform is useless. You cannot pass on what you do not have. A dead branch cannot produce living fruit. Matthew 7:18

Restoration requires the original source. Joseph Smith did not take the existing churches and improve them. He received authority directly from heaven — from John the Baptist, from Peter, James, and John — beings who held the authority in the primitive church. He did not inherit it from any existing church. He received it from God. That is restoration.

Acts 3:19–21 calls it "the restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began." Restitution — not reformation. The word itself demands a prior loss. This Church is the restitution. Other Christian bodies are reformations of a reform. The difference is not trivial — it goes to the question of divine authority.

The Lord declared through Joseph Smith that this was "the only true and living church upon the face of the whole earth."D&C 1:30 Not the only sincere church. Not the only well-meaning church. The only one with His full authority, His complete ordinances, and His living direction. The Restoration is real — or it is nothing.

missionary.simple: Reform takes something existing and corrects it. Restoration requires the original source. Joseph Smith did not improve existing churches — he received authority directly from heaven to restore what was lost.
missionary.explain: Luther reformed. Calvin reformed. Every Protestant denomination is a reform of something before it. But you cannot pass on authority you do not have. Joseph Smith received it from heaven — from John the Baptist and from Peter, James, and John.
missionary.why: If the authority was never lost, every church has a valid claim to it. If it was lost — as the apostasy scriptures clearly prophesy — then reform is useless. Only restoration works.
missionary.invite: Would you like to know the difference between what existing churches offer and what a restored church means for you personally?

questions.question: 130. Why do you believe that the First Vision in 1820 is the most important event since the resurrection?
questions.answer:
Because it ended the apostasy. For nearly 1,800 years, the heavens were closed. No new revelation. No living apostles. No restoration of the priesthood. No authorised ordinances. The entire Christian world was operating on tradition, uninspired interpretation, and the remnants of truth preserved in a Bible that had itself been through the hands of uninspired translators and councils.

Then in the spring of 1820, in a grove of trees in New York, a 14-year-old boy asked God which church to join — and God the Father and His Son Jesus Christ appeared to him. For the first time since the apostasy, a prophet stood face to face with the living God. The relationship between God and man that had been severed was restored. Heaven spoke again.

That is not a small event. That is the turning point of human history after the resurrection. Everything that followed — the restoration of the priesthood, the organisation of the Church, the coming forth of the Book of Mormon, the gathering of Israel, the building of temples, the sealing of families — all of it flows from that morning in the grove.

Amos 3:7 — the Lord does nothing without first revealing it to His servants, the prophets. Before the restoration could begin, the Prophet had to be called. Before the Prophet could be called, the Father and the Son had to appear. The First Vision was not just an answer to a boy's prayer. It was the signal that the restoration of all things — promised by every prophet from the beginning — had begun.

Joseph recorded the vision: "I saw two Personages, whose brightness and glory defy all description, standing above me in the air. One of them spake unto me, calling me by name and said, pointing to the other — This is My Beloved Son. Hear Him!"Joseph Smith—History 1:17 Heaven spoke. The dispensation opened. Everything changed.

missionary.simple: It ended nearly 1,800 years of silence between God and man. For the first time since the apostles, a prophet stood face to face with the living God. The restoration began that morning.
missionary.explain: 'Everything that followed — the Book of Mormon, the priesthood, the temple, the gathering of Israel — flows from that one morning in a grove of trees in 1820. God broke the silence.'
missionary.why: You are living in the dispensation that the First Vision opened. Every blessing of the restored gospel is available to you because of what happened that spring morning.
missionary.invite: Would you read Joseph Smith's account of the First Vision and ask God what it means for you personally?

questions.question: 163. If Joseph Smith was a prophet, why are there multiple differing accounts of his First Vision?
questions.answer:
There are multiple accounts of Paul's vision on the road to Damascus as well. Acts 9:7 says the men with Paul "stood speechless, hearing a voice, but seeing no man." Acts 22:9 says they "heard not the voice of him that spake." One says they heard a voice, the other says they did not. By that same measure, Paul would be a false apostle.

There are four gospel accounts of the resurrection — Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John — and they differ in details. Different women, different angels, different sequences. By that same reasoning, the resurrection never happened — a conclusion no serious reader accepts. Multiple accounts from different perspectives and different purposes naturally emphasise different elements.

Joseph Smith told the story of his vision in different settings, at different times, for different audiences. A personal journal entry written for his own reflection will differ in emphasis from a formal history written for public record. That is not contradiction. That is the natural human pattern of recounting a significant experience.

The core of every account is the same. He saw a light. He saw two personages. He was told his sins were forgiven. He was told not to join any existing church. The names of the Father and the Son vary in their explicit appearance across accounts — some focus on Christ, some mention both. But the event is consistent. The experience is consistent. The fruits are consistent.

No one has ever found a single account of the First Vision that contradicts the core testimony. What some call contradictions are differences in detail and emphasis — the same kind of differences that exist in the gospel accounts of Christ's own life.

missionary.simple: Multiple accounts of the same event naturally emphasize different things for different audiences. Paul's conversion has two accounts in Acts that differ in detail. The resurrection accounts in the four gospels differ. That is normal.
missionary.explain: 'Every account of the First Vision is consistent on the core facts: a light, two personages, his sins forgiven, told not to join any existing church. Differences in emphasis are not contradictions — they are how human memory works.'
missionary.why: The same standard that would dismiss Joseph Smith's vision would dismiss Paul's conversion and the resurrection accounts. Honest inquiry applies the same standard to all.
missionary.invite: Would you read Joseph Smith's 1838 account of the First Vision and ask God whether his testimony is true?

questions.question: 169. Is it true that Joseph Smith married girls as young as fourteen?
questions.answer:
The historical record shows that Joseph Smith was sealed to Helen Mar Kimball at the age of fourteen. This is a fact we acknowledge. It is also one of the most frequently cited and least carefully examined facts in much critical literature about the Church.

The 19th-century context is critical. Marriage at young ages was common in 19th-century America, and the practice was understood by participants as an eternal covenant rather than a conventional marriage in every modern sense. Helen Mar Kimball's own father, Heber C. Kimball — an apostle of the Church — gave his daughter to Joseph. He understood the principle of eternal sealing. Helen herself wrote about it in later life, confirming the sealing and stating that she understood it as an eternal covenant rather than a conventional marital relationship.

Whether the sealing included a marital relationship in the conventional sense is debated even among historians. Many of Joseph's plural marriages appear to have been primarily eternal or dynastic sealings — covenants that established family connections for eternity — rather than conventional marriages in every sense.

We do not defend the practice by modern cultural standards. We contextualise it within the revelation given to Joseph Smith and within the historical period in which it occurred. The God who gave Abraham a child at a hundred, who commanded Hosea to marry a harlot, and who sent His people into the wilderness for forty years does not always operate by the conventions of the culture His servants live in. We trust the revelation. We acknowledge the complexity of the history.

missionary.simple: Helen Mar Kimball was sealed to Joseph at fourteen. We acknowledge this. The 19th-century legal marriage age was ten to twelve in most states. Historical context matters and we do not hide it.
missionary.explain: 'The sealing appears to have been primarily an eternal covenant rather than a conventional marriage. We do not defend every aspect of early Church history — but we do not let complexity drive us away from the truth that has been confirmed.'
missionary.why: God has always worked through imperfect people in imperfect times. The question is not whether every detail of history is comfortable — it is whether the Book of Mormon is true and the priesthood is real.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to ask God directly whether Joseph Smith was a prophet — and trust that answer over every historical complexity?

questions.question: 204. Why did Joseph Smith join the Freemasons just weeks before introducing the Temple Endowment?
questions.answer:
Joseph Smith himself offered the explanation — that Masonry had preserved, in distorted form, fragments of the ancient temple ceremonies going back to Solomon's temple. He joined Masonry not to borrow from it but because he recognised in it the corrupted remnant of something originally divine.

The timing argument assumes that proximity equals derivation. By that logic, every biblical prophet who lived among pagan cultures and then revealed sacred ordinances was simply copying the pagans. Moses grew up in Egypt, surrounded by Egyptian religious ceremony. The rituals he established for Israel bear certain structural similarities to Egyptian religious practice. Does that mean Moses borrowed from Pharaoh? No. It means that some elements of true worship were preserved in corrupted form across many cultures — and God restored them in their purity through His prophet.

Joseph Smith's explanation is consistent with what we know about ancient Near Eastern religion. Scholars have identified clear parallels between the temple endowment and ancient Egyptian initiation texts — texts that were not accessible to Joseph Smith in 1842 and were not translated until decades after his death. If he borrowed from Masonry, how did he independently replicate Egyptian papyri that the world had not yet read? The answer is that he did not borrow. He restored.

missionary.simple: Joseph Smith joined the Freemasons in 1842 because he recognized in their ceremonies the corrupted remnants of ancient temple ordinances going back to Solomon. He then received the endowment by revelation in its restored, complete form.
missionary.explain: 'Scholars have found that the endowment parallels ancient Egyptian initiation texts that Joseph could not have accessed in 1842. If he borrowed from Masonry, how did he independently match texts the world had not yet translated? He did not borrow. He restored.'
missionary.why: The similarities between Masonry and the temple are evidence of a common ancient divine source — not evidence that Joseph invented anything.
missionary.invite: Would you like to understand what actually happens in temple ordinances and why millions of members testify that they are sacred, not manufactured?

questions.question: 205. Is it true that Joseph Smith ran for President of the United States in 1844?
questions.answer:
Yes. Joseph Smith declared his candidacy for the Presidency of the United States in 1844. He did not do it with any expectation of winning. He did it because he had a platform of genuine political and social reform that no existing candidate was offering.

His platform included the abolition of slavery — paid for by the sale of public lands. The reform of prisons from punishment-based to rehabilitation-based. The reduction of Congress's size and salary. The establishment of a national bank with checks against the manipulation of currency that was devastating frontier economies.

He was not a marginal figure. He commanded a large and organised following. His militia — the Nauvoo Legion — was the second largest armed force in the United States. His voice carried national weight. His candidacy gave the saints a political presence and a platform for ideas that were decades ahead of their time.

He was killed at Carthage Jail in June 1844 — two months after formally announcing his candidacy. His death ended the campaign. But the positions he took — particularly on slavery — were positions that cost him his life in a country where powerful men had already decided he had to be silenced. He was not a man who sought safety. He was a man who spoke truth and accepted the consequences.

questions.question: 11. If your church is true, why are there so many other churches claiming truth too?
questions.answer:
The existence of many churches does not prove that no church is true. It shows that men have divided what was once one. Paul wrote, “Now I beseech you, brethren... that there be no divisions among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment.”1 Corinthians 1:10 He wrote that because divisions had already appeared. His correction proves the original standard was unity.

Christ established one church. He gave it apostles, prophets, teachers, and a single doctrine. Ephesians 4:4–5 "One Lord, one faith, one baptism." Not hundreds. One. The multiplying of churches is not a sign of God's blessing — it is evidence of the apostasy that Paul and Peter both foresaw.

Peter warned of false teachers. 2 Peter 2:1 Paul warned of grievous wolves. Acts 20:29–30 John warned that the beast would make war with the saints. Revelation 13:7 All of them saw the falling away coming. The presence of many conflicting churches is exactly the condition they described — not proof that all churches are equal, but proof that restoration was needed. And restoration is what God sent — through the prophet Joseph Smith — in fulfillment of scriptures written centuries before he was born.

missionary.simple: Yes. Joseph Smith declared his candidacy in 1844 not to win, but to put a platform of genuine reform before the nation — including the abolition of slavery decades before the Civil War.
missionary.explain: 'He was killed two months after announcing his candidacy. Men who wanted him silenced feared both his influence and his message. That is not the biography of a fraud.'
missionary.why: Understanding the historical record of Joseph Smith shows a man of courage, vision, and prophetic foresight — not a deceiver.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to study Joseph Smith's life and ask God whether his character is consistent with someone God would call as a prophet?

questions.question: 23. Why do you claim to be the only true church?
questions.answer:
We do not make that claim out of pride. We make it because Christ made it first. He said, "I will build my church" — not churches. Matthew 16:18 Paul wrote, “One Lord, one faith, one baptism.”Ephesians 4:5 Not many faiths. One.

Christ organized His church with apostles, prophets, teachers, elders, priests, and deacons — a specific structure for specific purposes. Ephesians 2:20; 4:11 If that structure is necessary, then any organization missing it is not the church He built.

We do not say that good people cannot be found in other churches. We do not say that sincere believers will be condemned. We say that the authority, the organization, the priesthood, and the ordinances of the original church were lost through apostasy — and that they have been restored.

Roger Williams — considered the founder of the Baptist Church — wrote that there was "no regularly constituted church of Christ on earth, nor any person authorized to administer any church ordinance, nor can there be until new apostles are sent by the great head of the Church, for whose coming I am seeking." He knew what was missing. We have what he was seeking.

missionary.simple: We say it because Christ said it first. He said 'I will build my church' — not churches. Paul said one Lord, one faith, one baptism. That is the standard — and it is met here.
missionary.explain: We are not saying good people cannot be found elsewhere. We are saying that the authority, the priesthood, the ordinances, and the organization of Christ's original church were lost and have been restored here.
missionary.why: Roger Williams — founder of the Baptist Church — said no regularly constituted church of Christ existed on earth. He knew what was missing. We have what he was seeking.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to ask God directly — with a sincere heart — whether this is His restored church?

questions.question: 42. Why do you believe the original church fell away? Christ promised it would never fail.
questions.answer:
He promised that the gates of hell would not prevail against His church. Matthew 16:18 The question is — which church? The church built on the rock of revelation. He said so in that same verse. He was not promising institutional survival. He was promising that the principle of revelation — and the church built upon it — would ultimately prevail.

And it has. It was restored. But first, the apostasy that Paul, Peter, and John all warned about took place.

Paul told the Thessalonians, “Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first.”2 Thessalonians 2:3 What falling away? The very apostasy of the church. He wrote to Timothy, “The time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine.”2 Timothy 4:3 He told the Ephesian elders, “After my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.”Acts 20:29

John saw a beast that “made war with the saints, and overcame them.”Revelation 13:7 He saw an angel flying through the heavens with the everlasting gospel to preach to the inhabitants of the earth. Revelation 14:6 If the gospel was already on the earth, why did an angel have to bring it back?

The Lord himself can preserve His people through apostasy and restore what was lost. That is not failure. That is His plan. He promised restoration in Acts 3:19–21 — a "restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began."

missionary.simple: Paul, Peter, and John all prophesied a falling away. It happened. The restoration is not evidence that God failed — it is evidence that God kept His promise to restore all things.
missionary.explain: Paul told the Thessalonians that Christ would not come again until there was a great falling away first. That apostasy happened. God preserved the scriptures through it. And then He restored everything else through Joseph Smith.
missionary.why: Acts 3:19–21 promises the restitution of all things before Christ's second coming. We are living in that restitution. The restoration was not an accident — it was promised.
missionary.invite: Would you read 2 Thessalonians 2:1–3 and Acts 3:19–21 and ask God what they mean for today?

questions.question: 51. Why do you believe the Apostasy happened? Didn't God protect His church?
questions.answer:
God protected the principle of truth. He did not override the agency of men who chose to corrupt it.

He protected Noah — yet permitted the flood. He protected Israel — but still allowed the captivity. He protected Christ — but still allowed the crucifixion. Protection does not mean prevention of all evil. It means the ultimate purpose of God will not be defeated.

Paul warned the Ephesian elders with tears: “For I know this, that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock. Also of your own selves shall men arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away disciples after them.”Acts 20:29–30 That is not a hypothetical. That is a prophecy of what would happen.

He wrote to Timothy, “The time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears.”2 Timothy 4:3 He wrote to the Thessalonians that before Christ's return, “there come a falling away first.”2 Thessalonians 2:3 The word translated "falling away" in Greek is apostasia — apostasy. Paul used the word himself.

Christ promised that after the falling away there would be a restoration. Acts 3:19–21 calls it "the restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began." A restitution only happens after a loss. The loss was the apostasy. The restitution is this work.

missionary.simple: God protected the principle of truth but did not override human agency to corrupt it. He protected Noah through the flood — but still allowed the flood. Protection does not mean prevention of all evil.
missionary.explain: 'Paul prophesied the apostasy directly — using the word apostasia. He said it had to come before Christ's return. God allowed it because men chose it. He promised to restore it, and He did.'
missionary.why: The apostasy was not God's failure — it was His plan unfolding exactly as prophesied. The restoration is the fulfillment of every ancient promise.
missionary.invite: Would you read 2 Thessalonians 2:1–3 and Acts 3:19–21 together and ask God what they mean for today?

questions.question: 66. Why do you preach that the original church was lost? That makes God look powerless.
questions.answer:
It does not make God powerless. It reveals the agency of men. God does not force men to be faithful. He never has. He gave Israel His law and they made a golden calf at the foot of Sinai while Moses was still on the mountain. He gave them the Promised Land and within a generation they were worshipping Baal. He sent His own Son and they crucified Him. God is not powerless — men are rebellious.

Paul was not speaking hypothetically when he wept and warned the Ephesian elders that "grievous wolves" would enter the flock after his departure. Acts 20:29 He was prophesying a reality he knew was coming. He told the Thessalonians the Lord would not come again until “there come a falling away first.”2 Thessalonians 2:3 He told Timothy that men would “heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears.”2 Timothy 4:3

These are not warnings about small problems. They are prophecies of a wholesale departure from the truth. And they were fulfilled. The result is what we see today — hundreds of churches, none agreeing, none with apostles, none with living prophets, none with the authority to bind on earth and in heaven, none performing the ordinances as they were delivered.

God preserved the scriptures. He preserved the principle of truth. And when the time was right — the "dispensation of the fulness of times" Paul spoke of in Ephesians 1:10 — He restored everything through His prophet. That is power. That is not failure.

missionary.simple: The apostasy happened because men chose to corrupt the truth. God did not force them to be faithful. He allowed their agency and then — exactly as He promised — restored everything through His prophet.
missionary.explain: 'Paul told the Ephesian elders that grievous wolves would enter the flock after his departure. That is not a hypothetical warning — it is a prophecy. It happened exactly as he said.'
missionary.why: Understanding the apostasy is not an attack on sincere Christians. It is the explanation of why a restoration was necessary and why it matters.
missionary.invite: Would you like to understand what was lost in the apostasy and what has been restored — and why it changes everything?

questions.question: 89. Why do you say Christ organized a church? Couldn't people just follow Him personally without an organisation?
questions.answer:
He organised one. That is not our claim alone — it is the record of the New Testament itself. He chose twelve apostles. Matthew 10:1–4 He gave them specific titles, specific powers, and specific responsibilities. He called seventies and sent them out two by two. Luke 10:1 He established bishops, elders, priests, teachers, and deacons. Ephesians 4:11; 1 Timothy 3:1–13 He gave keys of authority to Peter. Matthew 16:19 He organised a church — deliberately, specifically, with a structure that matched every office Paul described in his epistles.

When people tried to follow Christ outside that organisation — like Simon who wanted to purchase the power of the Holy Ghost — they were corrected by the apostles. Acts 8:18–23 The organisation mattered. The authority within it mattered. You could not separate the truth of Christ from the structure He appointed to carry it.

Paul wrote that without apostles and prophets, people are “tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine.”Ephesians 4:14 That is not a picture of personal freedom. It is a picture of spiritual chaos. The organisation is not a cage. It is the framework within which the gifts of God are properly administered, the ordinances are properly performed, and the people are properly guided to eternal life.

missionary.simple: Christ organised one church with specific offices, specific authority, and specific ordinances. You cannot separate His truth from the structure He appointed to carry it.
missionary.explain: 'When Simon tried to buy the power to give the Holy Ghost, Peter corrected him immediately. The organisation mattered. You could not separate the gospel from the authority behind it.'
missionary.why: The church Christ built is not an optional structure for people who like organized religion. It is the vessel He appointed to carry His ordinances and authority.
missionary.invite: Would you like to compare the church Christ organised in the New Testament to what you see in the restored Church today?

questions.question: 110. Why do you believe the Church today needs to match the exact structure of the primitive church?
questions.answer:
Because Paul said so in Ephesians 4:11–14 — apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers were given for the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ — "till we all come in the unity of the faith." We are not at that unity. Therefore the offices are still needed.

He did not say some of these offices are needed. He did not say the ones you find convenient are needed. He said these offices were given — all of them — for a specific purpose that has not yet been achieved. The conclusion is clear: if you still need the purpose, you still need the means Christ appointed to achieve it.

The Church of Christ founder Alexander Campbell stated that the church should speak where the Bible speaks and be silent where the Bible is silent. Very well. The Bible speaks of apostles and prophets as necessary until the unity of faith is achieved. Is there unity of faith today? Then apostles and prophets are necessary. Where is the Church that has them? We are standing in front of you.

Every office listed in the New Testament — apostle, prophet, elder, bishop, priest, teacher, deacon, seventy — exists in this Church today. Name another church that makes that claim. If no one else can produce the structure Christ ordained, they cannot claim to be the church He built.

missionary.simple: Paul said the church needs apostles and prophets until we reach unity of the faith. We are not at that unity — 800 churches disagree on everything. So apostles and prophets are still needed.
missionary.explain: 'If you still need the purpose, you still need the means Christ appointed to achieve it. Paul did not say apostles were needed only for the first century. He said they were needed until unity — and we do not have unity.'
missionary.why: Living apostles and prophets are not a nice addition to the gospel. They are the structure Christ built it on — and without them, the church drifts into confusion.
missionary.invite: Would you like to meet our current apostles and prophets — see what they teach — and ask God whether they hold real authority?

questions.question: 124. Why do you use the word "Mormon"? Isn't the correct name the Church of Jesus Christ?
questions.answer:
The correct name — the name given by revelation — is “The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.”Doctrine and Covenants 115:4 Christ himself directed it in 1838. "Why is it called by any other name? I have asked men, and they have told me it was the church of Christ, or the church of God... but I say unto you... my church shall be called in these last days even The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints."

Christ said, “Ye shall call the church in my name.”3 Nephi 27:8 His name it bears. His church it is.

"Mormon" came from the outside. Those who opposed us first used the name. The name stuck. We do not object to it in casual conversation — but we are clear about what the Church is named and why. The Book of Mormon is named after the prophet-compiler Mormon, who edited and compiled the ancient record. Members who believe in that book came to be called Mormons by association.

The correct name is clear. We are members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Not the Church of Joseph Smith. Not the Mormon Church. The Church of Jesus Christ — named by Him, organised by Him, directed by Him through living prophets, and built on His atonement, His resurrection, and His priesthood authority.

missionary.simple: The correct name — given by revelation — is The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Mormon is a nickname from outsiders that stuck. His name it bears. His church it is.
missionary.explain: 'Christ himself directed the name in 1838. He asked why it was called anything other than His name. His church bears His name because it is His church.'
missionary.why: The name is not a branding decision. It is a statement of ownership and authority. This is Christ's church — named by Him and built on His priesthood.
missionary.invite: Would you like to know what it means for a church to literally belong to Jesus Christ and bear His name?

questions.question: 125. Why do you believe that only your church has the fullness of the gospel? Other churches have parts of it.
questions.answer:
We agree that other churches have parts of it. We do not say that other churches have nothing. They have the Bible. They have testimony of Christ. They have the practice of prayer and charity and worship. Many have sincere and good people.

But having parts of the truth is not the same as having the fullness. Paul wrote of "the dispensation of the fulness of times" in which God would “gather together in one all things in Christ.”Ephesians 1:10 Fulness. Not parts gathered together. All things in one. That gathering of all truth into one restored church is what Joseph Smith was called to accomplish.

Christ said He would build His church — one church, with one baptism, one faith, one Lord. Ephesians 4:4–5 Not several churches, each with part of the picture. He organised one structure with all the offices Paul described. That structure either exists intact or it does not. If it exists — with apostles, prophets, priesthood authority, living revelation, and all the ordinances — it is here. If it does not exist in any organisation, then no organisation is the Church Christ built.

We are not saying every person outside this Church is lost. We are saying every person outside this Church is missing something they need. And the merciful God who provided it will judge every soul by the light they received and the truth available to them. But for those who find it and reject it, the responsibility increases. We offer the fullness. The choice is yours.

missionary.simple: Other churches have parts of the truth — the Bible, testimony of Christ, sincere faith. What they are missing is the authority, the priesthood, the ordinances, and the fulness of the gospel.
missionary.explain: 'We are not saying every person outside this Church is lost. We are saying every person outside this Church is missing something God wants to give them. We offer the fulness — not the criticism.'
missionary.why: The fulness of the gospel includes the ordinances that bind on earth and in heaven. Partial truth is precious — but fulness is what God promised and what He has restored.
missionary.invite: Would you like to understand specifically what the restored fulness includes that you may not yet have received?

questions.question: 127. Why do you teach that the Church of Christ was fully organised before Pentecost?
questions.answer:
Because the evidence in the Gospels demands it. Christ said in Matthew 18:17, "Tell it unto the church." He used the present tense. At that point, before the cross, before the resurrection, before Pentecost — He referred to an existing church as the forum for resolving disputes. If the Church was not yet organised, who was He telling people to tell?

He gave Peter the keys of the kingdom of heaven in Matthew 16:19 — before Pentecost. He ordained the twelve apostles and gave them power and authority in Matthew 10 — before Pentecost. He sent the seventy in Luke 10 — before Pentecost. He told His disciples in Luke 17:20–21 that "the kingdom of God is among you" — present tense, before Pentecost.

What happened at Pentecost was the giving of the Holy Ghost in its fullness — a new dimension of spiritual power that the Church had not yet received. But the structure, the authority, the organisation — these existed before. Peter did not organise the church at Pentecost. He proclaimed the name of Christ, administered baptism, and conferred the Holy Ghost on those who came in.

Pentecost was the empowerment of an already-organised church — not its birth. The distinction matters because it shows that the pattern of the Church follows Christ's organisation, not the dramatic experiences of any single day.

missionary.simple: Christ used the word church before Pentecost, organised the twelve before the cross, and gave Peter the keys before the resurrection. The church existed before Pentecost.
missionary.explain: 'Christ told people to tell it to the church — present tense — before He died. He gave Peter the keys of the kingdom before the resurrection. Pentecost was the empowerment of an already-organised church, not its birth.'
missionary.why: Understanding this shows that the church Christ built had a clear structure from the very beginning — the same structure that has been restored today.
missionary.invite: Would you like to trace the organisation of Christ's church from the Gospels and see how precisely it matches what we have today?

questions.question: 129. Why do you say that the Catholic Church is in apostasy? They trace their authority to Peter.
questions.answer:
We say every church is in apostasy — including every Protestant denomination. The apostasy was universal. No institution escaped it, because the priesthood authority to perform the ordinances was held by living men who all died without the means to restore it from the earth.

The Catholic claim to Peter is worth examining carefully. Where in the Bible is there a record of Peter transferring his authority to a bishop of Rome? Where is the "transfer of authority" recorded in the scriptures? Paul, in his epistles, names the leaders of churches throughout Asia and Europe. He names Clement, Linus, and others. Not one of them is declared to hold the keys Christ gave to Peter. Not one of them is called an apostle with authority to bind on earth and in heaven.

The Catholic Church claims that tradition preserves what scripture does not record. But tradition in the hands of uninspired men produces exactly what Paul warned against — “divers and strange doctrines”Hebrews 13:9, the addition of commandments not from God, the subtraction of plain truths, and the substitution of human philosophy for divine revelation. The Papal claim of infallibility was not declared until the 19th century — nearly 1,800 years after Peter. That alone is sufficient evidence that the authority was not properly passed on.

Roger Williams — the founder of the Baptist Church — concluded the same thing. No regularly constituted church of Christ existed on the earth, he said, until new apostles were sent from heaven. He was right. And that sending happened through Joseph Smith.

missionary.simple: Every church is in apostasy — including every Protestant denomination. The authority was held by living men who all died without the power to restore it from the earth. Only God could restore it.
missionary.explain: 'The Catholic claim to Peter is not found in the New Testament. Paul named many church leaders — none of them is declared to hold Peter's keys. The chain was broken when the last apostle died.'
missionary.why: The question is not which church has the nicest people or the most sincere beliefs. The question is which church holds the authority Christ gave to His apostles.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to ask God — with a sincere heart — which church holds the authority He originally gave to Peter?

questions.question: 10. Why do you have a living prophet today? Isn't the Bible enough?
questions.answer:
Amos 3:7 tells us the Lord does nothing without revealing it first to His servants, the prophets. Not past prophets. Servants — present tense. If He is still working in the world, He is still speaking through prophets.

Ephesians 4:11–13 tells us that Christ gave the Church apostles and prophets to bring us to a unity of the faith. Are we at that unity today? Every sincere church reads the same Bible yet disagrees on nearly every doctrine. Unity has not been achieved. The need has not been met. Therefore, by Paul's own words, apostles and prophets are still needed.

Peter himself said Paul's writings were hard to be understood, and that the unlearned twisted them to their own destruction. 2 Peter 3:15–16 If the Bible alone could settle all questions, there would be one church. Instead there are hundreds, each with a different interpretation of the same text.

The Bible is the word of God. We revere it. But the God who gave us the Bible has not gone silent. He spoke to Moses. He spoke to Elijah. He spoke to Paul. He speaks today. To say He no longer does is to preach a god different from the one revealed in every page of that same Bible you are defending.

Nephi saw our day in vision and prophesied that the great and abominable church would take away from the gospel many parts that are plain and precious. 1 Nephi 13:26–29 The result was apostasy and confusion. The remedy was always the same: God restores His voice through living prophets. 2 Nephi 27:5–6

missionary.simple: Because the God who spoke through prophets has not gone silent. Amos said God does nothing without revealing it first. If God is still working, He is still speaking through living prophets.
missionary.explain: 'Look at 800 churches all reading the same Bible and disagreeing on everything. God is not the author of that confusion. Living prophets are how He settles doctrinal questions in every generation.'
missionary.why: You deserve a direct answer from God — through His appointed servants — not just your own interpretation of an ancient text.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to listen to a message from our living prophet and ask God whether he speaks with divine authority?

questions.question: 14. Why do you believe in modern revelation when the Bible says God does not change?
questions.answer:
Living prophets have taught that God's voice has not been silenced — He continues to speak to His children today. God does not change. His character, His mercy, His justice — these are eternal. But the statement "God does not change" has never meant that He stopped speaking. If it did, every prophet after Moses would be false. Every epistle Paul wrote would be unnecessary. The entire New Testament would be an unauthorized addition.

Malachi 3:6 says, "I am the Lord, I change not." That is a statement about His nature — His reliability, His trustworthiness. It is not a statement that He sealed His lips after the book of Revelation was compiled by uninspired men centuries later.

The same God who spoke to Abraham spoke to Moses. The same God who spoke to Moses spoke to Isaiah. The same God who spoke to Isaiah spoke to Peter. That pattern has never changed — because God does not change. He speaks to His servants in every dispensation.

If God truly does not change, then the God who sent prophets will always send prophets. The God who gave revelation will always give revelation. The God who organized a church with apostles and prophets will always have a church with apostles and prophets. That is the correct application of Malachi's words.

The Book of Mormon confirms: "For do we not read that God is the same yesterday, today, and forever, and in him there is no variableness neither shadow of changing? And now, if ye have imagined up unto yourselves a god who doth vary, and in whom there is shadow of changing, then have ye imagined up unto yourselves a god who is not a God of miracles."Mormon 9:9–10 An unchanging God keeps speaking.

missionary.simple: God does not change — His character, mercy, and reliability are eternal. But that same unchanging God has always spoken through prophets. If He stops speaking, that is the change.
missionary.explain: 'Malachi says God does not change. The same God who spoke to Abraham spoke to Moses. The same God who spoke to Paul speaks today. Consistency requires Him to keep speaking — not to go silent.'
missionary.why: A God who changes would stop speaking. The God who does not change keeps appointing prophets in every dispensation — including ours.
missionary.invite: If the same God who called Moses is still active today, how would you expect Him to communicate? Would you be willing to ask Him?

questions.question: 20. Why do Latter-day Saints follow a prophet instead of relying only on the Bible?
questions.answer:
We follow a prophet because that is exactly what the Bible teaches. Amos 3:7 — the Lord does nothing without revealing it to His servants, the prophets. Ephesians 4:11–13 — Christ gave the Church apostles and prophets to lead it until we reach unity of the faith. Acts 3:22 — Moses himself said the Lord would raise up a prophet like unto him, "and it shall come to pass that every soul which will not hear that prophet shall be destroyed."

We do not follow the prophet instead of the Bible. We follow the prophet because the Bible tells us to. The argument "follow the Bible, not the prophet" has no scripture behind it. Christ never said "after I am gone, rely only on this book." He said "I will build my church" on revelation, and He organized an apostleship.

The Bible was compiled, canonized, and handed down by uninspired men centuries after the apostles died. It has been translated, retranslated, and interpreted thousands of ways. If the Bible alone were sufficient, there would not be 800 churches reading it today and disagreeing on everything. We love the Bible. We read it. We defend it. But we trust the living God more than any tradition of men.

Nephi prophesied of a day when people would say: "A Bible! A Bible! We have got a Bible, and there cannot be any more Bible."2 Nephi 29:3 He then recorded the Lord's answer: "Know ye not that there are more nations than one? Know ye not that I, the Lord your God, have created all men?"2 Nephi 29:7 God speaks to all His children — not just through one record, and not without living representatives.

missionary.simple: Because the Bible itself tells us to follow living prophets. Amos, Paul, and Peter all taught that God speaks through prophets. We follow the prophet because the scriptures command it.
missionary.explain: 'Moses said the Lord would raise up a prophet and that every soul who does not hear him will be cut off. That is not our claim — that is Moses, speaking of Christ and of every prophet God appoints.'
missionary.why: Following a living prophet is not blind obedience — it is the same faith that following Moses or Peter required. The test is always the same: by their fruits.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to study the teachings of our living prophet and ask God whether what he teaches is true?

questions.question: 38. Why do you need living apostles today when we already have the writings of the original apostles?
questions.answer:
Paul addressed this in Ephesians 4:11–14. He said Christ gave apostles and prophets and teachers for the "perfecting of the saints" and to bring us to a "unity of the faith" — "that we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine." That is not a description of the first century only. That is a description of every century — including this one.

The writings of the original apostles are precious. We have them and we treasure them. But writings are not the same as living authority. In Acts 8, the people of Samaria had Philip and his preaching. They had scripture. But when Peter and John arrived, there was something they could do that Philip could not. They laid hands on the people and they received the Holy Ghost. Acts 8:17 That required a living apostle. You cannot lay hands through a page.

Timothy was told by Paul, “Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.”1 Timothy 4:14 That gift came through a living man's hands — not through reading a letter. The authority of the apostleship is conferred. It is transmitted. It cannot be inherited from a book.

The Bible itself tells us that without living apostles, the church drifts into every wind of doctrine. Look around and count the churches. That is the condition Paul warned us about — and it is exactly what we are living in.

The Book of Mormon records that Christ organised the same apostleship among the Nephites: he called twelve disciples, gave them authority, and instructed them to baptize and confer the Holy Ghost. 3 Nephi 18:36–37 The same Lord who organised His church in the Old World organised it in the New. The same Lord has organised it again today through living apostles who hold the same keys.

missionary.simple: Because the writings of apostles are not the same as the living authority of apostles. You cannot lay hands on someone through a page. Authority must be conferred by a living person who holds it.
missionary.explain: 'Timothy received his gift through the laying on of hands from Paul — a living apostle. He could not receive it by reading Paul's letter. The authority is transmitted in person, not in writing.'
missionary.why: The Bible is precious and true. But it cannot perform ordinances, lay on hands, or confer the Holy Ghost. Living apostles are still needed for those things.
missionary.invite: Would you like to understand the specific things that only living apostles and priesthood holders can do — and why that matters for your salvation?

questions.question: 48. Why does your church believe in continuing revelation when Jude said the faith was "once delivered to the saints"?
questions.answer:
Church apostles have taught that the God who spoke through prophets in ancient times is the same God who speaks through prophets today. Jude 1:3 says to "earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints." He was calling on members to defend the gospel against false teachers who had crept in. He was not saying the gospel was permanently sealed and no new word from God would ever come again.

If that interpretation were correct, then every scripture written after Jude's letter would also be unauthorized — including the Gospel of John, the Book of Revelation, and several of Paul's epistles, all of which were written after Jude. This shows that Jude was not sealing all future revelation, but defending the gospel already received against those who were distorting it.

The God of the Bible speaks. He spoke through Moses. He spoke through the prophets. He spoke through Christ. He spoke through the apostles. He spoke through John on Patmos in Revelation — long after most of the New Testament was written. The idea that He then went permanently silent is a tradition of men with no scriptural support.

Amos 3:7 is still in the Bible. "The Lord God will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets." That verse has never been repealed. Until it is, we have every reason to expect God to keep speaking — and every reason to believe He is doing so through His living prophet today.

missionary.simple: Jude was telling believers to defend the gospel against false teachers — not declaring that God would never speak again. Every scripture written after Jude's letter would be eliminated by that reading.
missionary.explain: 'If Jude 1:3 sealed all future revelation, then the Book of Revelation — written after it — would also be unauthorized. Jude was defending the gospel against false teachers, not declaring that God would never speak again.'
missionary.why: God has never been silent for long. He speaks in every dispensation through appointed servants. The restoration is His latest and greatest speaking.
missionary.invite: Would you read Amos 3:7 and ask God whether He is speaking through living prophets today?

questions.question: 68. Why do you believe God speaks through dreams and visions today?
questions.answer:
Because Moses said He would. Numbers 12:6 — "If there be a prophet among you, I the Lord will make myself known unto him in a vision, and will speak unto him in a dream." That is not a description of what God used to do. It is a description of how God speaks to prophets — permanently.

Joel prophesied, “Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions.”Joel 2:28 Peter stood on the day of Pentecost and declared that prophecy was being fulfilled. That prophecy was only partially fulfilled at Pentecost. The last half — signs in the heavens and the great day of the Lord — has not yet come. Partial prophecies have multiple fulfilments.

The God of the Bible spoke in dreams to Abraham, Jacob, Joseph, Daniel, and the apostle John. He gave Paul a vision on the road to Damascus. He gave Peter a vision on the rooftop. He appeared to Stephen. He appeared to the apostles after His resurrection. The same God does the same things. A God who stopped speaking in visions and dreams is not the God of the Bible. That god is the creation of men who ran out of prophets and decided to call it complete.

missionary.simple: Because Moses said God communicates through visions and dreams to His prophets — permanently, not just in ancient times. Joel prophesied your sons and daughters would prophesy in the last days.
missionary.explain: 'Peter quoted Joel's prophecy on the day of Pentecost. But the signs in the heavens and the great day of the Lord described in Joel had not yet come. Partial prophecies have multiple fulfillments — including one for our day.'
missionary.why: We are living in the very days Joel prophesied. Dreams, visions, and revelation are not past-tense gifts — they are active gifts for those who seek them.
missionary.invite: Have you ever had a dream or experience that felt like more than coincidence? Would you like to know what God says about how He speaks to His children today?

questions.question: 95. Why do Latter-day Saints say their testimony is a spiritual witness? Isn't that just emotion?
questions.answer:
Paul wrote, “The natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.”1 Corinthians 2:14 Spiritual things are discerned spiritually. The tools of natural reasoning alone cannot reach them.

Peter did not discover that Jesus was the Christ through logic or evidence. Christ told him plainly: “Flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.”Matthew 16:17 The revelation came from God — not from a process of argument. That is the pattern.

Emotion and spiritual witness are not the same thing. Emotion rises and falls with circumstances. A spiritual witness through the Holy Ghost is anchored in truth — it produces peace, clarity, and a settled knowledge that does not fade with time or difficulty. Paul called it “the peace of God, which passeth all understanding.”Philippians 4:7 It passes understanding — meaning it cannot be produced by understanding alone.

Alma compared it to planting a seed. (Alma 32.) When the seed of truth is planted in the heart, it swells, it grows, it produces fruit. The fruit is the evidence that the seed was good. Millions of people from every nation, culture, and background have planted that seed and received that witness. Their testimony is not emotion. It is the Holy Ghost bearing record — exactly as He was promised to do.

missionary.simple: A spiritual testimony is not emotion — it is a witness from the Holy Ghost that something is true. Peter did not figure out who Christ was by logic. The Father revealed it to him directly.
missionary.explain: 'Paul said the natural man cannot receive the things of the Spirit because they are spiritually discerned. That is not anti-intellectual — it is recognizing that there is a category of knowledge that only God can give.'
missionary.why: You can know spiritual truth with the same certainty as anything else — but the method is different. It requires sincerity, real intent, and faith in Christ.
missionary.invite: Have you ever prayed sincerely about something spiritual and felt a response? Would you be willing to try Moroni's promise and see what God says?

questions.question: 98. Why do you believe that obedience to the prophet is required? Can't members think for themselves?
questions.answer:
We think for ourselves constantly. Every member of this Church made a personal choice to be baptized, to receive a testimony, and to follow the prophet. The prophet does not compel anyone. He cannot. God Himself does not compel. “I give unto men weakness that they may be humble.”Ether 12:27 Humility, not compulsion, is the operating principle.

But Christ said, “He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.”Matthew 10:40 Receiving the prophet is not blind obedience. It is the same faith that receiving any servant of God has always required. When Israel refused to receive Moses, they wandered forty years. When Judah refused to receive Jeremiah, Jerusalem fell. When the world refused to receive Christ, the apostasy followed.

The test of discipleship has always been whether a person is willing to obey when they do not fully understand. Abraham was asked to sacrifice Isaac. He did not understand it. He obeyed. And the Lord provided another way. That is the pattern — obedience opens the door to understanding. Understanding alone does not open the door to the blessings God reserves for the obedient.

We also believe in personal revelation. Every member has the right to receive spiritual confirmation of the prophet's teachings. We do not ask anyone to follow blindly. We ask them to seek confirmation from God — and trust that the God who called the prophet will also confirm it to the humble seeker.

missionary.simple: Members think for themselves constantly. Every member chose to be baptized personally. Following a prophet is the same faith that following Moses or Peter required — it is not blind obedience.
missionary.explain: 'We do not ask anyone to follow blindly. We ask them to seek their own confirmation from God — and trust that the God who called the prophet will also confirm it to the humble seeker.'
missionary.why: Real discipleship has always required trusting God's appointed servants even when you do not fully understand. That is what makes it faith.
missionary.invite: Would you be willing to ask God personally whether our prophet speaks with divine authority — and trust the answer you receive?

questions.question: 123. Why does your church teach about the second coming with such specificity? Doesn't Christ say no one knows the hour?
questions.answer:
Christ said no one knows the day or the hour — not the angels, not the Son, only the Father. Matthew 24:36 That is still true. We do not know the day. We do not know the hour. But we are commanded to know the season.
